title: Velvety Argentinian Beef Skillet
description: A grassy, juicy skillet of marinated beef cooked with Marsala wine, perfect for a brunch-mood meal.
instructions: Heat a cast‑iron skillet over medium heat until shimmering, then add the olive oil.
When the oil is hot, sear the beef strips in a single layer for about 2 minutes each side until browned.
Remove the beef from the skillet and set aside on a plate.
Pour the Marsala wine into the skillet, scraping up any browned bits, and reduce by half, about 3 minutes.
Add the beef broth, smoked paprika, oregano, thyme, sea salt, and pepper; stir and bring to a gentle simmer.
Return the beef to the skillet, fold in the butter, and cook, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the meat reaches medium‑rare, about 4–5 minutes.
Stir in the chopped parsley and lemon juice, taste, and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve immediately, spooning the sauce over the meat, and enjoy your velvety Argentinian breakfast skillet.
